excerpt: These fourteen rak'ahs indicate the fourteen limits arising from the command of the invitation, signifying the confluence of two epochs
source_location_hint: Prayer of the Fifteenth of Sha'ban, cosmological interpretation

excerpt: The night divides Sha'ban into two equal halves and bisects the three sacred months, indicating the transition from external to esoteric revelation
source_location_hint: Night of the Fifteenth of Sha'ban, exoteric-esoteric correspondence

excerpt: On this night the Prophet's external proclamation concluded and the keys of the esoteric teaching were entrusted to Ali
Shatr denotes one half; the second half surpasses the first, placing the nights of power in the latter half where esoteric teaching stands higher
source_location_hint: Symbolic hierarchy of exoteric and esoteric dimensions

excerpt: Rajab stands singular among the four sacred months, called Rajab al-Farid; it is isolated as the letter ra without dots, distinct from conjoined months
source_authority: Numerical and alphabetic symbolism of sacred months
source_location_hint: The singular station of Rajab al-Haram

excerpt: On the first day of Rajab, Noah embarked upon the Ark by divine command and was instructed to make all who accompanied him fast in honor of Allah
source_location_hint: First Night of Rajab, primordial covenant and fasting

excerpt: The Prophet appeared after twenty-seven Imams; the remaining three days signal three Imams (Abd al-Muttalib, Abu Talib, Abdullah) preceding his birth, and on this day he received the universal human nature
source_location_hint: Twenty-seventh of Rajab, prophetic birth and imamic succession

excerpt: Fatima bint Asad, mother of Ali, embodies the Gate of Gates through her son who represents the sum of all primary perfections; the Imam gathers all believers, limits, and forms of supplication
source_authority: Feminine gnosis and maternal imamic principle
source_location_hint: First Friday of Rajab and the station of Fatima bint Asad
